cross_scene_arcs.title: Moretti's Ascension to Negotiator Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Moretti transitions from a peripheral law enforcement presence to the central authority managing the hostage crisis, establishing direct communication with Sonny and asserting control over both tactical operations and public perception.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Moretti positions himself as the dominant local authority, distinguishing himself from the FBI.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Moretti is fully established as the lead negotiator, having extracted critical intelligence and initiated trust-building measures with Sonny.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Coordination failures with surrounding officers threaten to undermine the trust Moretti has built with the hostage-taker.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Moretti's Confidence and Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As public order collapses and internal pressures mount, Moretti's outward authority is challenged by his own self-doubt and jurisdictional friction with the FBI, revealing a deepening internal and institutional conflict.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Moretti asserts operational control by manipulating environmental conditions and media access.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Moretti expresses personal fear of making a fatal mistake and is challenged by Sheldon, signaling a crisis of confidence and command.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The extent of Moretti's actual authority and the consequences of potential errors remain unaddressed, with ethical accountability deferred through dark humor.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Leon-Sonny-Moretti Emotional Triangle
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Moretti becomes entangled in the emotionally charged bond between Sonny and Leon, attempting to manipulate their relationship to achieve a peaceful resolution while being destabilized by its complexity and intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Moretti is shocked by the revelation that Leon claims to have married Sonny, complicating his understanding of the crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Moretti assumes full control of communication, intervening directly when Leon fails to act, positioning himself as the sole mediator between the lovers.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Leon's mental instability and emotional paralysis leave the effectiveness of Moretti's strategy uncertain, and the truth of the marriage remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Containment to Confrontation: Moretti's Tactical Escalation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Moretti shifts from passive negotiation to active psychological pressure, using isolation tactics and direct confrontation to force Sonny into submission as peaceful avenues begin to fail.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Moretti initiates direct dialogue with Sonny via bullhorn, seeking to build trust and extract demands.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Moretti cuts power and communication lines, isolating the bank and compelling Sonny to respond to a public summons under coercive conditions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains uncertain whether Sonny will surrender or resist violently when directly confronted under these heightened conditions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moretti's Moral and Institutional Duality
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Moretti navigates a dual role as both a loyal institution man and a disillusioned observer of systemic decay, torn between hierarchical loyalty and personal moral judgment as chaos erodes the rule of law.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Moretti receives intimate affirmation from the Commissioner, reinforcing his place in the police hierarchy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Moretti witnesses public and officer misconduct during the crisis, deepening his disillusionment despite maintaining operational control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral weight of potential civilian casualties is acknowledged but dismissed, leaving ethical accountability unresolved within the institution.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Enforcer's Burden: Sal's Role as Operational Backbone
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sal consistently assumes the role of the disciplined, vigilant enforcer responsible for maintaining internal control during the heist, transitioning from preparation to active execution and ultimately becoming the sole armed authority in the bank.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal meticulously prepares his appearance and mindset for the heist, embodying readiness and psychological investment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sal becomes the primary armed presence ensuring compliance, with full responsibility for hostage control during Sonny's absence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his operational effectiveness, Sal remains emotionally isolated, and the division of labor between him and Sonny creates underlying tension about leadership cohesion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Identity Under Siege: Sal's Struggle Against Misrepresentation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sal's identity is repeatedly constructed, challenged, and misrepresented—by Sonny, the media, and law enforcement—triggering his attempts to assert agency over how he is perceived, especially regarding his sexuality and credibility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sonny labels Sal as 'the killer' and implies a homosexual relationship to manipulate police perception.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The FBI seeks to isolate Sal, exploiting the narrative of him as a separate, more dangerous entity, further alienating him from Sonny.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sal's demand for correction of the media's portrayal is only partially acknowledged, leaving his identity distorted and his sense of self undermined.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Trauma and the Pact: Sal's Unyielding Stance Against Imprisonment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sal's absolute refusal to surrender stems from deep trauma rooted in childhood sexual abuse in prison, driving his commitment to the suicide pact and creating escalating tension with Sonny's wavering resolve.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal learns the robbery is televised and reaffirms his refusal to accept a plea deal, citing the pact to die rather than return to prison.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sonny reveals Sal's traumatic past, exposing the psychological foundation of his inflexible stance, while Sal remains silent but resolute.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sonny's potential betrayal of the escape plan and Sal's dependence on him create a volatile emotional undercurrent that threatens the unity of their partnership.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Discipline and Dignity: Sal's Rituals of Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Throughout the crisis, Sal maintains personal discipline and moral rigidity as a means of asserting control and preserving dignity, culminating in a fleeting moment of human connection before his death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal meticulously dresses and checks his appearance before entering the bank, symbolizing psychological preparation and self-possession.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After receiving Maria's rosary, Sal briefly softens, showing vulnerability and hope—only to be abruptly killed, cutting short his potential transformation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The spiritual recognition Sal receives contrasts with his violent end, leaving unresolved whether his disciplined persona was a shield or a path to redemption.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Partnership: Shifting Power Between Sal and Sonny
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The dynamic between Sal and Sonny evolves from synchronized partnership to growing emotional distance, marked by moments of shared authority, miscommunication, and ultimately tragic separation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal and Sonny react in unison to the blackout, demonstrating their shared command and interdependence during the crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sal is carried dead past Sonny, marking the irreversible end of their bond and the collapse of their shared dream.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sonny is left emotionally shattered, unable to process Sal's death, while Sal dies without knowing whether Sonny would have honored their escape plan.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Robbery to Rescue: Sonny's Shifting Goal
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sonny's objective evolves from executing a successful bank robbery to orchestrating a complex escape plan involving a jet and limousine, ultimately collapsing into a desperate bid for survival and symbolic closure. The initial goal of securing money gives way to ensuring Leon's future and escaping with Sal, culminating in a failed flight and Sonny's emotional and physical defeat.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: To fully secure the bank, prevent outside detection, and safely access and collect the vault and teller drawer cash without triggering alarms.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Arrested, manacled, and stripped of all agency; reduced to a passive onlooker as Sal's body is removed and the hostages move on without him
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legal and emotional validity of Sonny's will remains uncertain, as does whether Leon will receive the support he intends or if his loved ones will ever fully understand his actions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Bond: Sonny and Leon's Unresolved Relationship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sonny's emotional core centers on his deep, unconventional love for Leon, whose presence and rejection define his psychological arc. The relationship shifts from a private motivation to a public spectacle, then collapses under the weight of miscommunication, emotional distance, and Leon's ultimate rejection, leaving Sonny isolated despite fulfilling his promise.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sonny's goal remains securing Leon's freedom and ensuring his future, now intensified by the traumatic collapse of their attempted reunion.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite completing his mission, Sonny remains isolated, as the hostages respond with detachment and formality, underscoring the emotional cost of his actions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The separation between Sonny and Leon remains unhealed, with no guarantee of future contact or safety for Leon.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise and Fall of Authority: Sonny's Status Transformation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sonny ascends from a disorganized robber to a charismatic, media-savvy leader commanding public admiration and internal control, only to descend into powerlessness and dehumanization. His status shifts from criminal to folk hero to broken captive, reflecting the volatility of power in a media-fueled crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sonny remains in control but shows increasing strain; he attempts to address the physical discomfort of the hostages by seeking to restore the air conditioning, indicating a growing sense of responsibility for their well-being.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He is dehumanized, manacled, and passively processed by authorities, symbolizing total loss of agency and the conclusion of his mission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sonny's lingering emotional attachment to Sal and the hostages remains unreciprocated, highlighting his isolation and the futility of his actions.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Internal and External Conflict: Trust, Betrayal, and Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sonny faces escalating internal and external conflicts, including distrust among accomplices, deception within his own group, and manipulation by law enforcement. His struggle to maintain control is undermined by betrayal, suspicion, and the FBI's tightening grip, culminating in his forced compliance and loss of autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sonny's goal shifts from unified control of the situation to suspicion and self-preservation as he senses betrayal.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite Sonny's insistence, Sheldon overrules him and reinstates Agent Murphy as the driver, maintaining FBI control over the operation while Sonny is forced to comply.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Sheldon is actively betraying Sonny or merely asserting police authority remains unclear, leaving Sonny uncertain and vulnerable.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Legacy Over Survival: Sonny's Emotional and Moral Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As survival becomes unlikely, Sonny shifts focus from escape to legacy, confronting his mortality and seeking emotional closure. He reveals his past, expresses love for his family, and formalizes his commitment to Leon's future, transforming from a criminal into a tragic figure seeking redemption through sacrifice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sonny shifts from resisting capture to preparing for death, indicating he no longer expects to survive the standoff and is now focused on securing a meaningful legacy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He achieves emotional closure, publicly declaring his love and responsibilities, and symbolically completes his transformation from criminal to tragic figure making peace with his life.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legal validity and fate of the will remain uncertain, as do the ultimate fates of Leon, Heidi, and Sonny's relationships beyond his death.
